landing.tagline:
No-equipment training.
Draw a card, do the set.

setup.classicDesc: Your own pace, total time is tracked.
setup.challengeDesc: Every card has a clock. Clear them all.

workout.hint: Done? Tap the card — the next one's already waiting.
workout.dailyFooter: {count} cards · same deck for everyone · new deal tomorrow
workout.saveFailed: Saving isn't working right now — this workout may not appear in your history.

results.guestNote: Guest results aren't saved. Create an account to track progress over time.

progress.title: Progress
progress.streakCaption: streak · jokers up your sleeve this week: {freezes}
progress.empty:
No workouts yet.
Finish one and it shows up here.

auth.successNote: Check your email to confirm your account before logging in.
auth.guestBanner: You banked {points} points at the guest table — make an account and they're yours for keeps, streak and rank included.

classic.explanation: Your pace, no pressure — only total time is tracked.

perfect_deck.explanation: Every card has its own time quota. Move to the next card before the quota runs out and the card is beaten ✓. Quota expired? The card is lost ✗ — but the workout never stops: finish your reps at your own pace and carry on. Unused time doesn't carry over. Beat every card for a PERFECT DECK. Your first run is against our estimate; once you set a record, you race your best time +5%.

sprint.title: 🏃 Blitz
sprint.desc: 3, 5 or 10 minutes. Deal till the clock dies.
sprint.explanation: Fixed time — shuffle the deck and flip cards until the clock runs out. Every cleared card still earns points; the cards-cleared counter is just your pace.

court.title: 👑 The Court
court.desc: 16 royals: J, Q, K — and the Ace to catch your breath.
court.explanation: Per-card deadline like Perfect Deck, but you always play against our estimate — not your record. ×1.25 bonus on points. Aces are deliberately included as "rest" cards — Ace = 1 rep.

survive.title: ⏳ On the Clock
survive.desc: A bank of seconds. Empty bank — game over.
survive.explanation: You start with a bank of seconds. Every finished card refills the bank with that card's quota (estimated time for the card). How far can you get through 52 cards? Pausing doesn't drain the bank. Beat every card for a ×1.5 bonus.

daily.title: 🎲 Daily Deal
daily.desc: One deck, 20 cards, same for everyone.
daily.explanation: Every day a new 20-card deck, the same for all players. Exercises and level are set by the day — no choices. Per-card deadline like Perfect Deck, always against our estimate. Your first attempt of the day counts for the chip and record; replaying the same day is a replay.

streak.title: Workout streak
streak.explanation: 🔥 Your streak grows for every day you clear a deck. 🃏 Jokers up your sleeve — two a week. Skip a day and one plays itself, the flame stays lit. Fresh pair every Monday, they don't stack.

entry.title: How are you playing today?
entry.quickDesc: Pick your stakes, we build the deck.
entry.customDesc: Every exercise, every card — rig it your way.
entry.challengeDesc: Five tables. Records on the line.

points.label: Points
points.guestKeep: You earned {points} points — create an account to keep them.
points.formula: Points = sum of (reps × exercise factor) for every finished card. Factor: tier Ⅰ ×1, tier Ⅱ ×1.5, tier Ⅲ ×2. In challenge modes a final multiplier boosts the total — up to ×2.

xp.label: XP
xp.explanation: XP is the sum of points from every finished workout — Quick Deal and Stack the Deck count too, not just Challenge. It only grows. 14 ranks from The Joker (0) to King (105,000) — see How to Play for the full ladder.

ranksDesc.r0: Everyone starts wild. About two workouts and you're an Ace.
ranksDesc.r1: Lowest card in the deck — but you're IN the deck now.
ranksDesc.r2: Two decks a week and it's becoming a habit.
ranksDesc.r4: Steady hands. The streak barely flickers.
ranksDesc.r6: Clean runs are your normal now.
ranksDesc.r7: Luck had nothing to do with it.
ranksDesc.r8: A pace most players never hold.
ranksDesc.r11: First royal. You have real mileage behind you.
ranksDesc.r12: Elite of the deck — quotas fall, streaks hold.
ranksDesc.r13: The crown. Hard to win, defended with every deck.

howToPlay.title: How to Play
howToPlay.deckIsTrainerDesc: Every card is an exercise — the number is your rep count. Do the work, tap the card, next one's dealt.
howToPlay.suitsExplainer: A standard deck holds 52 cards and jokers. Suits are muscle groups: ♥ push, ♣ pull, ♠ legs, ♦ core. The Ace is 1 — the lightest card — and the royals carry J=11, Q=12, K=13. The deck runs empty, the session ends — time and points go straight to your history and XP.
howToPlay.streakText: Streak — the flame grows every day you clear a deck. Three skipped days in one week and it goes out.
howToPlay.jokersText: Jokers up your sleeve — two a week. Need a rest day? One plays itself and the flame stays lit. Fresh pair every Monday — they don't stack.
howToPlay.aboutText: SHUFFLE is free — training app made to promote healthy lifestyle

quickDeal.short: Pick your stakes, we build the deck.
quickDeal.long: You pick the stakes and the deck size — we build the deck. No clocks here: Quick Deal and Stack the Deck run without them — just you and the stopwatch.

stackTheDeck.short: Every exercise, every card — rig it your way.
stackTheDeck.long: Pick the number of cards (12–52) and the rep multiplier (0.5×–2×). A taller stack pays more XP. Your setup is saved for next time.

challenge.short: Five tables. Records on the line.
challenge.long: Five modes: Perfect Deck, Blitz, The Court, On the Clock and Daily Deal. Clocks, runs and records — this is where the XP is made.

jokerMode.short: A guided breather when a joker turns up.
jokerMode.long: When a joker turns up: 30 seconds of guided breathing — in, out. The stopwatch keeps running and your run stays alive. Shorter decks carry one, longer ones carry two.

profile.title: Profile
profile.jokersDesc: Two a week. Skip a day and one plays itself — the flame stays lit. Fresh pair every Monday.
profile.guestCta: Create an account to track your rank, streak and history.

jokers.title: 🃏 Joker
jokers.explanation: Draw a joker and you get 30 seconds of rest instead of an exercise — it moves on to the next card automatically. Shorter decks (12 cards) carry one joker, longer ones carry two. It never costs your per-card time or budget — rest is always free.
